perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is for a term (the "Term") of 12 months from the Effective Date on June 1s t 2020 and expiring May 31st 2021. (a) In the case that the company would not like to extend the terms of agreement for an additional month. The company must notify the consultant within 5 days of the conclusion of the 12 month term. Without notification the contract will automatically extend for an additional month of service. Upon termination, Consultant agrees to perform the necessary information transfer required at the time.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is for a term (the "Term") of 12 months from the Effective Date on June 1st, 2020 and expiring May 31st, 2021. (a) In the case that the company would not like to extend the terms of agreement for an additional month. The company must notify the consultant within 5 days of the conclusion of the 12-month term. Without notification the contract will automatically extend for an additional month of service. However, either party may terminate this agreement with 30 days written notice. Upon termination, Consultant agrees to perform the necessary information transfer required at the time.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. Originally, the contract automatically extends for an additional month unless the company provides a 5-day notice before the term ends. The added clause allows either party to terminate with 30 days written notice, which conflicts with the automatic extension and creates uncertainty about the termination process.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is for a term (the "Term") of 12 months from the Effective Date on June 1st, 2020 and expiring May 31st, 2021. (a) In the case that the company would not like to extend the terms of agreement for an additional month. The company must notify the consultant within 5 days of the conclusion of the 12-month term. Without notification the contract will automatically extend for an additional month of service. However, either party may terminate this agreement with 30 days written notice. Upon termination, Consultant agrees to perform the necessary information transfer required at the time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by adding a 30-day termination clause. The original text stipulates automatic contract extension for an additional month unless a 5-day notice is provided. The contradicted text is located within the changed text. The added termination clause directly conflicts with the automatic extension clause, creating uncertainty and inconsistency about the termination process. It's unclear whether the automatic extension still applies or if the 30-day notice overrides it.

perturbation.original_text: For services rendered hereunder during the term, the Consultant shall receive: A cash pre-payment of US$150,000 which Consultant acknowledges it received directly from an investor participating in the Company's March 2020 convertible debenture offering and that no additional cash payment is due and payable under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: For services rendered hereunder during the term, the Consultant shall receive: A cash pre-payment of US$150,000 which Consultant acknowledges it received directly from an investor participating in the Company's March 2020 convertible debenture offering and that no additional cash payment is due and payable under this Agreement. However, Consultant will be entitled to additional quarterly bonuses dependent on performance, as defined by the Board of Directors.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that no additional cash payment is due but then adding that the Consultant will be entitled to additional quarterly bonuses, creating an ambiguity regarding payment terms. This contradicts the initial statement that no additional cash payment is due, creating potential for disputes about the consultant's compensation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For services rendered hereunder during the term, the Consultant shall receive: A cash pre-payment of US$150,000 which Consultant acknowledges it received directly from an investor participating in the Company's March 2020 convertible debenture offering and that no additional cash payment is due and payable under this Agreement. However, Consultant will be entitled to additional quarterly bonuses dependent on performance, as defined by the Board of Directors.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating that 'no additional cash payment is due' and then adding 'Consultant will be entitled to additional quarterly bonuses'. This directly conflicts with the initial statement, creating uncertainty regarding the consultant's compensation as now there is entitlement to additional payment. Therefore, a contradiction exists, making the text inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: All knowledge and information of a proprietary and confidential nature relating to the Company which the Consultant obtains during the Consulting period, from the Company or the Company's employees, agents or Consultants shall be for all purposes regarded and treated as strictly confidential for so long as such information remains proprietary and confidential and shall be held in trust by the Consultant solely for the Company's benefit and use and shall not be directly or indirectly disclosed in any manner whatsoever by the Consultant.
perturbation.changed_text: All knowledge and information of a proprietary and confidential nature relating to the Company which the Consultant obtains during the Consulting period, from the Company or the Company's employees, agents or Consultants shall be for all purposes regarded and treated as strictly confidential for so long as such information remains proprietary and confidential and shall be held in trust by the Consultant solely for the Company's benefit and use and shall not be directly or indirectly disclosed in any manner whatsoever by the Consultant. However, the Consultant may disclose such information if required by law or legal process, or with prior written consent of the CEO.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by initially stating that all confidential information shall not be disclosed in any manner, but then adding exceptions that allow disclosure if required by law or with the CEO's written consent. This creates uncertainty about the extent of confidentiality obligations, as the exceptions significantly weaken the initial absolute prohibition against disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All knowledge and information of a proprietary and confidential nature relating to the Company which the Consultant obtains during the Consulting period, from the Company or the Company's employees, agents or Consultants shall be for all purposes regarded and treated as strictly confidential for so long as such information remains proprietary and confidential and shall be held in trust by the Consultant solely for the Company's benefit and use and shall not be directly or indirectly disclosed in any manner whatsoever by the Consultant. However, the Consultant may disclose such information if required by law or legal process, or with prior written consent of the CEO.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating that confidential information shall not be disclosed in any manner, but then adding exceptions that allow disclosure if required by law or with the CEO's written consent. The contradicted text is exactly the same, however, this doesn't mean there isn't a contradiction, since the altered change explicitly contradicts with the previous text from 'shall not be directly or indirectly disclosed in any manner whatsoever' which is contradicted by 'However, the Consultant may disclose such information if required by law or legal process, or with prior written consent of the CEO.', thus generating a strong in-text contradiction.
